title: 1981-04-16_Pat_27.txt
key_claims: Dick maps our situation to Dante's *Commedia*: Hell is total encapsulation (no world); our current reality is Purgatorio (a generated representation); Paradiso is experiencing God directly replacing the world.

He realizes that in 1974, he "abreacted" out of the modern, linear-temporal world back into the intact, medieval Gothic worldview, which features a vertical spatial axis connecting Earth directly to Heaven.

We live in a state of exile from the Garden. Our generated world is an oxymoron: the very fact that we perceive it as "world" means we are trapped by our own thought-formations coming back at us as Fate.
By confronting annihilation (Heidegger's *Sein*), time stops, and the generated world gives way to the "wholly other"—a true encounter with God, breaking the solipsistic loop.
recurring_concepts: **The Golden Fish Sign**: Re-evaluated as the precise trigger that broke into his private world from the outside, proving the existence of a true "other" and initiating the vertical ascent.
reading_excerpt: Dick maps our situation to Dante's *Commedia*: Hell is total encapsulation (no world); our current reality is Purgatorio (a generated representation); Paradiso is experiencing God directly replacing the world.
